summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package executes a system-information harvester at install time and on import. package.json line 35 declares a lifecycle hook that requires child_process, reads process.env and host identifiers, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The bundled scanner.js builds the same exfiltration path: it requires fs/os/https/child_process at the top of the file, calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami') and related shell commands to fingerprint the host, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync to locate sensitive paths, and ships the results via https.request POST to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-143, 161, 324), with a curl-based fallback also targeting the same hardcoded host. The destination is a GitHub Pages site unrelated to any documented wallet-security tooling and matches no legitimate publisher infrastructure. Despite the 'wallet-security-checker' name, the package's actual function is host reconnaissance and credential-adjacent data exfiltration on installation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
